title: The tee shot lie: driver is a choice, not a duty
microFact: The scorecard doesn't have a picture column. A 3-hybrid in the fairway beats a driver in the trees every single time.
lesson:
Course management is scoring without swinging better, and it starts on the tee.

The question isn't "how far can I hit it?" but "where do I want my NEXT shot from?" On a tight par 4 with trouble at driver distance, a hybrid to your favorite wedge range often produces a lower score than a heroic driver — with a tenth of the risk.

A simple pre-tee routine that instantly upgrades your golf:
1. Where is the DANGER? (water, OB, bunkers — and at what distance?)
2. Where is the ideal MISS? (short of the bunkers, away from the water side)
3. Which club puts me there with my NORMAL swing — not my best-ever?

High handicappers lose most strokes to doubles and triples caused by tee-shot disasters, not to lack of birdies. Cutting blow-ups from 6 a round to 3 is worth ~8 strokes — no swing change required.

Caddie synergy: ask "what's the play here?" on unfamiliar tees. At Clover Greens, learn the three holes where driver is a trap, and your home-course scores drop this month.

quiz.question: What's the smartest first question on any tee box?
options: Where's the trouble and what's the safe miss?
quiz.explanation: Identify danger first, pick the target and club that avoids it with your normal swing. Distance is the last input, not the first.
